meta.educational_goal: To simulate the main building blocks of HTTPS using the Web Crypto API.

states.label: Idle
entry_actions: log('Demo initialized. Steps: 1) Generate CA → 2) Create Server & Certificate → 3) Verify → 4) Handshake → 5) Send Message. Try tampering and MITM tests.')
evidence: log('Demo initialized. Steps: 1) Generate CA → 2) Create Server & Certificate → 3) Verify → 4) Handshake → 5) Send Message. Try tampering and MITM tests.')

states.label: CA Generated
entry_actions: log('CA generated and added to client's trust store. CA public key (JWK) shown on the right.')
evidence: log('CA generated and added to client's trust store. CA public key (JWK) shown on the right.')

states.label: Server Created
entry_actions: log('Server certificate created for "example.com" and signed by CA. Certificate shown to the right.')
evidence: log('Server certificate created for "example.com" and signed by CA. Certificate shown to the right.')

states.label: Certificate Verified
entry_actions: log('Certificate verification: OK ✔ (signature valid and certificate within validity period)')
evidence: log('Certificate verification: OK ✔ (signature valid and certificate within validity period)')

states.label: Handshake Performed
entry_actions: log('Handshake successful — both sides derived the same AES-GCM session key. (Simulated ECDH)')
evidence: log('Handshake successful — both sides derived the same AES-GCM session key. (Simulated ECDH)')

states.label: Message Sent
evidence: log('Client -> Server (ciphertext, iv shown b64)')

states.label: Certificate Tampered
entry_actions: log('Certificate was tampered with (subject modified). Verification will now fail until reissued.')
evidence: log('Certificate was tampered with (subject modified). Verification will now fail until reissued.')

states.label: MITM Simulated
entry_actions: log('Client correctly rejected the attacker's certificate ✖ (signature not from a trusted CA).')
evidence: log('Client correctly rejected the attacker's certificate ✖ (signature not from a trusted CA).')
btnGenCA.addEventListener('click', async () => { log('Generating CA key pair (ECDSA P-256)...'); ... })

events.description: Create a server and sign its certificate.
evidence: btnGenServer.addEventListener('click', async () => { if (!CA.privateKey) { log('Please generate CA first.'); return; } ... })
btnVerify.addEventListener('click', async () => { if (!server.cert) { log('Create a server certificate first.'); return; } ... })

events.description: Perform the handshake to derive a session key.
evidence: btnHandshake.addEventListener('click', async () => { if (!server.cert || !server.certVerified) { log('Server certificate must exist and be verified first.'); return; } ... })
btnSend.addEventListener('click', async () => { if (!handshakeState.clientDerivedKey || !handshakeState.serverDerivedKey) { log('Perform handshake first.'); return; } ... })
btnTamper.addEventListener('click', async () => { if (!server.cert) { log('No server certificate to tamper with.'); return; } ... })
btnMITM.addEventListener('click', async () => { if (!server.cert) { log('Create a legitimate server certificate first so we can compare.'); return; } ... })
log('CA generated and added to client's trust store. CA public key (JWK) shown on the right.')
log('Server certificate created for "example.com" and signed by CA. Certificate shown to the right.')
log('Certificate verification: OK ✔ (signature valid and certificate within validity period)')
log('Handshake successful — both sides derived the same AES-GCM session key. (Simulated ECDH)')
log('Client -> Server (ciphertext, iv shown b64)')
log('Certificate was tampered with (subject modified). Verification will now fail until reissued.')
log('Certificate verification: FAILED ✖ (signature invalid)')

expected_observables: Client correctly rejected the attacker's certificate
evidence: log('Client correctly rejected the attacker's certificate ✖ (signature not from a trusted CA).')
<button id="btnGenCA" class="primary">1. Generate CA (trusted)</button>
<button id="btnGenServer">2. Create Server & Certificate (CA signs)</button>
<button id="btnVerify">3. Verify Certificate (client)</button>
<button id="btnHandshake">4. Perform Handshake (derive session key)</button>
<button id="btnSend">5. Send Encrypted Message</button>
<button id="btnTamper">Tamper Certificate</button>
<button id="btnMITM">Simulate MITM (attacker CA)</button>
<input id="serverName" type="text" value="example.com">
<input id="plainMsg" type="text" value="Hello from client over 'HTTPS'!"><
